description: The inquiry gate shape — a DIFFERENT decision than the formal-chargeback contest/concede, because an inquiry (Stripe warning_needs_response: a pre-dispute retrieval/inquiry, e.g. Visa RDR / Amex/Discover inquiry) hasn't become a chargeback yet. The merchant's genuinely-distinct discretionary act is `resolutionPath`: (a) refund_to_prevent — issue a refund now, which RESOLVES the inquiry before it escalates, incurs NO dispute fee, and keeps it off the dispute-ratio; the merchant trades the refund amount for avoiding the fee + chargeback risk; or (b) respond_with_evidence — defend the inquiry with an evidence package (no refund), same hash-binding as the chargeback path. This is the VCR-thread realization that IS drivable on the Stripe SoR: Stripe does not expose VCR allocation-vs-collaboration as distinct actions and does not support pre-arbitration/arbitration, but inquiry-vs-chargeback is a real, distinct, drivable workflow. The decision carries networkReasonCode + vcrClass for the captured Visa classification, and feeAtStake so the economic trade-off is on-chain. The engine enforces resolutionPath-conditional evidence at structural validation (refund_to_prevent requires refundAmount + rationale; respond_with_evidence requires evidencePackageHash). GATE + SoD ARE ORCHESTRATOR-ENFORCED, NOT TYPE-ENFORCED (see README): gateMode:auto can override defaultGateMode:principal (F-791) and principal==performer self-commitment is not rejected (F-758). Route through the principal gate, never set gateMode:auto, and provision distinct performer+principal identities.

disputeId.description: Stripe Dispute id (du_...) of the INQUIRY this decision resolves (status warning_needs_response).

disputeRef.description: recordId of the chargeback-dispute-filed-v1 (disputeFlow=inquiry) this descends from.

vcrClass.description: Captured VCR class (see chargeback-dispute-filed-v1.vcrClass). Classification only — Stripe abstracts the workflow.

inquiryDueBy.description: Inquiry response deadline (Stripe evidence_details.due_by), carried so a late decision is observable on-chain.

feeAtStake.description: The dispute fee (and ratio impact) AVOIDED if refund_to_prevent resolves the inquiry before it escalates to a chargeback — the economic input to the decision. The merchant trades min(refund, exposure) against this.

resolutionPath.description: The authorized inquiry-resolution path. refund_to_prevent: refund now to close the inquiry, no fee. respond_with_evidence: defend with an evidence package, no refund.
Must equal the record's criteria.resolutionPath — the path the principal authorized.

refundAmount.description: For refund_to_prevent: the amount refunded to resolve the inquiry (major units).

rationale.description: For refund_to_prevent: why refunding beats defending (e.g. refund < feeAtStake + expected chargeback loss).

acknowledgedNoFee.description: For refund_to_prevent: attests the operator understands resolving the inquiry by refund avoids the dispute fee and keeps it off the dispute ratio.

evidencePackageHash.description: For respond_with_evidence: sha256:<hex> of the exact Stripe evidence object submitted to the inquiry.

uncategorizedText.description: Optional Stripe evidence.uncategorized_text. Test mode: winning_evidence / losing_evidence force the outcome.
